data.protocol_title: A Phase II, Randomized, Double‑Blind, Placebo‑Controlled Study
data.principal_investigator: Dr. Ananya R. Mehta, MD, PhD

1.main:
This protocol describes a Phase II, randomized, double‑blind, placebo‑controlled clinical trial designed to evaluate the safety, tolerability, and efficacy of XYZ‑101 in adult participants with moderate‑to‑severe asthma.

The study aims to determine whether XYZ‑101 improves clinical outcomes compared with placebo when administered over a 24‑week treatment period.
Approximately 160 participants will be recruited across multiple clinical sites in India. Eligible participants will undergo screening assessments, followed by randomization in a 1:1 ratio to receive either XYZ‑101 or placebo. Safety evaluations, pulmonary function testing, efficacy assessments, and patient‑reported outcome measures will be collected at predefined intervals throughout the study.

The trial will adhere strictly to the principles of ICH E6(R3) Good Clinical Practice, the Declaration of Helsinki, and all applicable regulatory and ethical requirements. Investigators are responsible for ensuring participant safety, accurate data collection, and full compliance with the approved protocol.

This protocol includes detailed sections on study rationale, objectives, eligibility criteria, methodology, investigational product management, safety reporting procedures, statistical analysis plan, and operational oversight mechanisms. Findings from this study will support further clinical development of XYZ‑101 for asthma management.

synopsis.Title: A Phase II, Randomized, Double‑Blind, Placebo‑Controlled Study
synopsis.Study Description:
This is a multicenter, Phase II, randomized, double‑blind, placebo‑controlled clinical trial designed to assess the efficacy and safety of XYZ‑101 in adult participants with moderate‑to‑severe asthma. Approximately 160 eligible participants will be randomized in a 1:1 ratio to receive either XYZ‑101 or placebo over a 24‑week treatment period, followed by a 4‑week safety follow‑up.
The study aims to evaluate improvements in asthma control, lung function, and reduction in exacerbations, along with comprehensive safety assessments. All trial procedures will adhere to the principles of ICH‑GCP, the Declaration of Helsinki, and applicable regulatory guidelines.
synopsis.Objectives: To evaluate the efficacy of XYZ‑101 in reducing the frequency of asthma exacerbations at Week 24 compared with placebo.
synopsis.Endpoints:
To assess improvements in lung function (FEV₁).
To evaluate changes in asthma control (ACQ‑7 score).
To assess safety and tolerability of XYZ‑101.
synopsis.Study Population: To explore changes in airway‑inflammation biomarkers (eosinophils, FeNO, IgE). To characterize pharmacokinetic parameters (Cmax, AUC). To evaluate changes in quality‑of‑life scores (AQLQ).
synopsis.Sites/Facilities:
The study will be conducted at multiple accredited clinical research centers equipped with:

Pulmonary function laboratories
Emergency care and resuscitation facilities
Trained clinical research staff
Secure systems for data management and drug accountability
synopsis.Study Intervention Description:
Participants will be randomized to receive either:

XYZ‑101 (Inhalation Suspension), administered once daily, or
Matching placebo
synopsis.Study Duration: Total Study Duration: Approximately 12 months (including start‑up, enrollment, treatment, and follow‑up)

overview.title: A Phase II, Randomized, Double‑Blind, Placebo‑Controlled Study
overview.coordinating_investigator: Dr. Ananya R. Mehta, MD, PhD
overview.expert_committee: Dr. N. Raghavan – Clinical Pharmacology Expert Dr. Latha Venugopal – Pulmonology Specialist
overview.investigators: Dr. Ananya R. Mehta – Principal Investigator Dr. Prakash S. Raman – Sub‑Investigator Dr. S. Revathi – Sub‑Investigator
overview.trial_sites: Chennai Clinical Research Institute, Chennai Apollo Research Center, Bengaluru MedLife Trial Unit, Hyderabad

primary: To evaluate the efficacy of XYZ‑101 in reducing the frequency of asthma exacerbations.
Especially in adults with moderate‑to‑severe asthma.
secondary: To assess improvement in lung function measured by FEV₁.
To evaluate changes in Asthma Control Questionnaire (ACQ‑7) scores.
To determine the safety and tolerability profile of XYZ‑101.
exploratory: To assess changes in biomarkers associated with airway inflammation.
To explore pharmacokinetic (PK) parameters of XYZ‑101.

primary: Change from baseline in annualized rate of asthma exacerbations at Week 24.
secondary: Mean change in FEV₁ from baseline to Week 24.
Change in ACQ‑7 score from baseline.
Number and type of treatment‑emergent adverse events (TEAEs).
exploratory: Levels of serum eosinophils, FeNO, and IgE.
Change in Asthma Quality of Life Questionnaire (AQLQ) scores.

flowcharts.caption: Study Flow Overview
flowcharts.description: A schematic representation of participant progression through the study, including screening, randomization (1:1), treatment period (24 weeks), follow‑up period (4 weeks), and early termination pathway.

inclusion.text:
Adults aged 18–65 years.
Documented diagnosis of moderate‑to‑severe asthma for ≥12 months.
points: FEV₁ between 40% and 80% of predicted value.
History of ≥2 asthma exacerbations in past 12 months.
Stable inhaled corticosteroid treatment for ≥4 weeks.

exclusion.text:
Diagnosis of other significant pulmonary diseases (e.g., COPD).
Use of biologics for asthma within last 3 months.
points: Participation in other clinical trials within previous 30 days.

team.investigator_desc: The Principal Investigator is responsible for overall study oversight, ensuring compliance with the protocol, ethical conduct, accurate documentation, and participant safety.
team.coordinator_desc: The Study Coordinator supports daily trial operations including participant scheduling, informed consent facilitation, data entry, site documentation maintenance, and coordinating monitoring visits.

synopsis_data.statistical_methods:
The primary efficacy analysis will use an intention‑to‑treat (ITT) population.
Exacerbation rates will be analyzed using a negative binomial regression model.
Continuous secondary endpoints such as FEV₁ and ACQ‑7 scores will be assessed using mixed‑effects models for repeated measures (MMRM).
Safety analyses will be descriptive and include all participants who received at least one dose of study medication.

details.gcp_statement: This clinical trial will be conducted in full compliance with the principles outlined in the International Council for Harmonisation (ICH) Guideline for Good Clinical Practice (E6 R2/R3), the Declaration of Helsinki, and applicable local regulatory requirements. All investigators agree to adhere to these guidelines to ensure the rights, safety, and well‑being of trial participants.
details.approval_statement: By signing this document, the undersigned acknowledge approval of this clinical trial protocol and agree to conduct the study in accordance with its provisions, the Sponsor’s standard operating procedures (SOPs), applicable regulatory requirements, and ICH‑GCP guidelines. No deviations or modifications to the protocol may be implemented without written approval from the Sponsor and Ethics Committee, except where necessary to eliminate immediate hazards to participants.
